summary: We are sprinting toward the September launch of the China Going-Global report (joint with IE) and the Knowledge website revamp, while simultaneously kicking off SEO-driven campaigns for Asia Star and Intelligent China—this week is about locking down designs, campaign routes, and media partnerships.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the China Going-Global report: Felix is drafting a one-pager for structure and promotion, the team is translating the Chinese bulk, and we are confirming launch events with Tech in Asia and Tyson in Singapore for mid-September. On the Knowledge revamp, Joker is iterating on the homepage design to combine elements from both versions, and we need to provide specific feedback by end of week. For program marketing, Asia Star and Intelligent China are the SEO focus: Joseph and Daisy are running parallel campaign routes (AI vs traditional) for Asia Star starting now, and we need to decide on the campaign route for Intelligent China. Urgent this week: expedite social media agency selection (likely Media Minds), send updated campus video to Shou Hu for final review, and process the PO for the CKGSB-EuroCham event. Coming up: Professor Mei Jianping's report distribution to global media, and the IE report translation must be capped at 50% of original for the September launch.

meetings.title: Knowledge Website Revamp Review
meetings.summary: Team reviewed Joker's knowledge website redesign, noting it's similar to current site with menu improvements. Discussed Joseph's alternative layout for more articles and indices, and agreed to provide specific feedback for iteration.

meetings.title: SEO and Program Prioritization
meetings.summary: Meeting to discuss SEO strategy for CKGSB programs, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs, and planning to audit existing conversion tracking.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on menu categorization, brochure updates, CES quotes, campus video, Asia Start articles, Davos press release, social media agency search, and IE report translation.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report with CKGSB on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, including op-eds, case studies, and launch events in Singapore.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Reviewed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional) and discussed new 'Intelligent China' program targeting foreigners, with focus on module one in Beijing.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in market operations.

decisions.text: Remove dedicated video section; videos will be dispersed by topic.
Keep knowledge menu centered, school menu at top left.
Show more articles on homepage, including pinned article and latest.
Bring indices (BCI, BSI) to knowledge homepage.
Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships for initial SEO focus.
Include Berkeley program as a third priority for audit phase.
Menu categorization by topics is correct; will keep professor analysis separate.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu needed; either incorporate 10 points or turn into 10-point article.
Zhu Yang's feedback is not useful; he is using ChatGPT; team will not spend too much time on it.
Media Minds likely best agency given small budget; expedite selection.
IE report translation should not exceed 50% of original; launch at Tech in Asia Singapore on Sept 15.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with main research from Professor Leeway and op-eds from both schools.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Target completion by early September for Singapore events.
Asia Start campaign will run two parallel routes for the first month: AI-based (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
Intelligent China program is a 12-day, three-module program targeting foreigners, with module one in Beijing.
Use the editorial design direction for Knowledge revamp
Keep the original event theme for CKGSB-EuroCham EV event
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: CKGSB-EuroCham event collaboration on EV

decisions.text: Use Flow for design of China Going Global report
